root_text.an4.48:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.an4.48:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā visākho pañcālaputto upaṭṭhānasālāyaṁ bhikkhū dhammiyā kathāya sandasseti samādapeti samuttejeti sampahaṁseti, poriyā vācāya vissaṭṭhāya anelagalāya atthassa viññāpaniyā pariyāpannāya anissitāya.
root_text.an4.48:1.3: Atha kho bhagavā sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhito yena upaṭṭhānasālā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi.
root_text.an4.48:2.1: “Ko nu kho, bhikkhave, upaṭṭhānasālāyaṁ bhikkhū dhammiyā kathāya sandasseti samādapeti samuttejeti sampahaṁseti poriyā vācāya vissaṭṭhāya anelagalāya atthassa viññāpaniyā pariyāpannāya anissitāyā”ti?
root_text.an4.48:2.2: “Āyasmā, bhante, visākho pañcālaputto upaṭṭhānasālāyaṁ bhikkhū dhammiyā kathāya sandasseti samādapeti samuttejeti sampahaṁseti poriyā vācāya vissaṭṭhāya anelagalāya atthassa viññāpaniyā pariyāpannāya anissitāyā”ti.
root_text.an4.48:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ visākhaṁ pañcālaputtaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an4.48:3.3: Sādhu kho tvaṁ, visākha, bhikkhū dhammiyā kathāya sandassesi samādapesi samuttejesi sampahaṁsesi poriyā vācāya vissaṭṭhāya anelagalāya atthassa viññāpaniyā pariyāpannāya anissitāyāti.

translation_text.an4.48:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.an4.48:1.2: Now at that time Venerable Visākha, Pañcāli’s son, was educating, encouraging, firing up, and inspiring the mendicants in the assembly hall with a Dhamma talk. His words were polished, clear, articulate, expressing the meaning, comprehensive, and independent.
translation_text.an4.48:1.3: Then in the late afternoon, the Buddha came out of retreat and went to the assembly hall. He sat down on the seat spread out,
translation_text.an4.48:2.1: “Mendicants, who was educating, encouraging, firing up, and inspiring the mendicants in the assembly hall with a Dhamma talk?”
translation_text.an4.48:2.2: “Sir, it was Venerable Visākha, Pañcāli’s son.”
translation_text.an4.48:3.1: Then the Buddha said to Visākha,
translation_text.an4.48:3.3: It’s good that you educate, encourage, fire up, and inspire the mendicants in the assembly hall with a Dhamma talk, with words that are polished, clear, articulate, expressing the meaning, comprehensive, and independent.
translation_text.an4.48:4.1: Though an astute person is mixed up with fools,
translation_text.an4.48:4.2: they don’t know unless he speaks.
translation_text.an4.48:4.3: But when he speaks they know,
translation_text.an4.48:4.4: he’s teaching the state free of death.
translation_text.an4.48:5.1: He should speak and illustrate the teaching,
translation_text.an4.48:5.2: holding up the banner of the seers.
translation_text.an4.48:5.3: Words well spoken are the seers’ banner,
translation_text.an4.48:5.4: for the teaching is the banner of the seers.”
